mnemonics: RTL: Register Transfer Language is the 'Recipe' for data movement.
Control Signals are like Traffic Lights for data buses.
keyPoints: A register transfer is a basic operation that copies the contents of one register to another.
Control signals determine when and which register transfers occur during a clock cycle.
Data paths are defined by the connections between registers, ALUs, and other components via shared buses.
commonMistakes: Assuming a transfer happens on every clock edge; it only occurs if the control signal is active/asserted.
Confusing the data path (the bus) with the control path (the signals that enable the transfer).
Forgetting that a bus can only carry one value at a time, so simultaneous transfers to the same bus cause conflict.
